title: Core Task Management
description: Build the core task management library: CRUD operations, status transitions, and input validation. All task functions should be tested and callable from main.
acceptance_criteria: All CRUD and validation functions implemented, tested, and callable from main

title: Task CRUD
description: As a developer, I want to create, list, and update tasks so that the board can be managed programmatically.
acceptance_criteria: CreateTask, ListTasks, and UpdateStatus exist, tested, and return correct results

title: Create task
description:
Implement CreateTask(b *Board, title string, priority Priority) (*Task, error) that adds a new task to the board.

Acceptance criteria:
- Assigns a unique sequential ID
- Sets status to StatusTodo
- Sets created_at to current time
- Returns error if title is empty
- Appends task to board's task list
acceptance_criteria: CreateTask returns a new task with unique ID, correct defaults, and error on empty title

title: List tasks with filtering
description:
Implement ListTasks(b *Board, status Status) []*Task that returns tasks matching the given status.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns all tasks when status is empty string
- Returns only matching tasks when status is specified
- Returns empty slice (not nil) when no tasks match
- Does not modify the original board
acceptance_criteria: ListTasks filters correctly by status and returns empty slice for no matches

title: Update task status
description:
Implement UpdateStatus(b *Board, taskID int, status Status) error that transitions a task to the given status.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns error if task ID not found
- Returns error if status is not a valid Status constant
- Updates the task's status field
- Updates the task's updated_at timestamp
acceptance_criteria: UpdateStatus transitions status correctly and returns errors for invalid inputs

title: Task Validation
description: As a developer, I want task inputs validated so that malformed data is rejected before being stored.
acceptance_criteria: ValidateTitle and ValidatePriority exist, tested, and return descriptive errors

title: Validate task title
description:
Implement ValidateTitle(title string) error that checks title constraints.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns nil for valid titles (1-200 characters)
- Returns error for empty string
- Returns error for titles exceeding 200 characters
- Returns error for titles that are only whitespace
- Error messages are descriptive
acceptance_criteria: ValidateTitle returns nil for valid titles and descriptive errors for invalid ones

title: Validate priority range
description:
Implement ValidatePriority(p Priority) error that checks priority is within valid range.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns nil for priorities 0 through 4
- Returns error for negative values
- Returns error for values above 4
- Error messages include the invalid value
acceptance_criteria: ValidatePriority returns nil for 0-4 and descriptive errors for out-of-range values

title: Persistence & Serialization
description: Add JSON serialization for board state and computed statistics. Enables saving/loading boards and reporting progress.
acceptance_criteria: Board can be serialized to JSON and deserialized back; statistics are computed correctly

title: JSON Serialization
description: As a developer, I want to serialize and deserialize boards to JSON so that state can be persisted to disk.
acceptance_criteria: MarshalBoard and UnmarshalBoard exist, tested, and round-trip correctly

title: Marshal board to JSON
description:
Implement MarshalBoard(b *Board) ([]byte, error) that serializes a board to JSON.

Acceptance criteria:
- Produces valid JSON output
- Includes all board fields (name, tasks)
- Includes all task fields (id, title, status, priority, timestamps)
- Returns error if board is nil
acceptance_criteria: MarshalBoard produces valid JSON with all fields and errors on nil board

title: Unmarshal board from JSON
description:
Implement UnmarshalBoard(data []byte) (*Board, error) that deserializes a board from JSON.

Acceptance criteria:
- Correctly restores all board fields
- Correctly restores all task fields
- Returns error for invalid JSON
- Returns error for empty input
- Round-trips with MarshalBoard (marshal then unmarshal produces equivalent board)
acceptance_criteria: UnmarshalBoard restores all fields and round-trips with MarshalBoard

title: Board Statistics
description: As a developer, I want computed statistics so that I can report board progress.
acceptance_criteria: CountByStatus and CompletionPct exist, tested, and return correct values

title: Count tasks by status
description:
Implement CountByStatus(b *Board) map[Status]int that counts tasks grouped by status.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns a map with counts for each status present
- Missing statuses are absent from map (not zero-valued)
- Returns empty map for board with no tasks
- Does not modify the board
acceptance_criteria: CountByStatus returns correct counts grouped by status

title: Completion percentage
description:
Implement CompletionPct(b *Board) float64 that returns the percentage of tasks in StatusDone.

Acceptance criteria:
- Returns 0.0 for board with no tasks
- Returns 0.0 when no tasks are done
- Returns 100.0 when all tasks are done
- Returns correct percentage for mixed statuses (e.g., 2 of 4 done = 50.0)
- Depends on CountByStatus for counting
acceptance_criteria: CompletionPct returns correct percentage for all cases including empty boards

title: Retrospective & Parking Lot
description: Low-priority backlog items for future consideration. Not expected to be implemented during the current campaign.
acceptance_criteria: Items evaluated and either promoted to real work or archived

title: Consider database backend
description:
Evaluate whether a SQLite or similar database backend would be beneficial for larger boards. Currently in-memory only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document pros/cons of database backend
- Recommend go/no-go for implementation

title: Evaluate web UI framework
description:
Research lightweight Go web frameworks for a potential board UI. Currently CLI only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Compare at least 3 frameworks (e.g., templ, htmx+Go, Bubble Tea TUI)
- Recommend preferred approach
acceptance_criteria: Written comparison of 3+ frameworks with recommendation
